title: Java AOT Object Caching
description: Review Java 26 GC-agnostic ahead-of-time object caching.
learningGoals: Recognize that Java 26 can stream cached objects from a GC-agnostic AOT format.
Use -XX:+AOTStreamableObjects when a training run should force streamable cached objects.

completionChecks.prompt: What problem does Java 26 GC-agnostic AOT object caching address?
choices: It lets cached objects work across garbage collectors instead of one GC-specific layout
It replaces every class file with Java source
It turns off all background materialization
It removes the need for class loading
completionChecks.explanation: JEP 516 stores cached object references in a neutral form so the cache can be used with different collectors, including ZGC.

completionChecks.prompt: Which flag explicitly requests streamable GC-agnostic cached objects?
completionChecks.explanation: -XX:+AOTStreamableObjects asks the JVM to create a streamable, GC-agnostic object cache even when compressed ordinary object pointers are enabled.

completionChecks.prompt: What does the lesson's GC-agnostic note mean for ZGC?
choices: ZGC can be considered for production with an AOT cache instead of being excluded by object-reference layout
ZGC becomes the only supported collector
ZGC disables all AOT cache data
ZGC compiles Java source files at link time
completionChecks.explanation: The point is compatibility across collectors. Java 26 avoids tying the cached object layout to a single collector's in-memory references.
